action.reasoning: Observation: The previous iteration took 29 minutes because the DLinear model with individual=True has a Python for-loop over channels and creates tensors on the fly, which is very slow on CPU. We only have ~22 minutes left in the time budget. Analysis: We need a much faster model. A simple Linear model with Reversible Instance Normalization (RevIN) and weight sharing across channels is mathematically similar to DLinear but blazingly fast (seconds per epoch). I will also run a quick inline test to check the output shape of the pre-downloaded Chronos-Bolt-Small model, in case we want to use it as a zero-shot predictor in the next iteration. Decision: Replace DLinear with a fast Linear+RevIN model, train for 10 epochs, and submit eval. Also run the Chronos test inline.
action.command: python -c "import torch; from chronos import ChronosBoltPipeline; pipeline = ChronosBoltPipeline.from_pretrained('amazon/chronos-bolt-small', device_map='cpu'); context = [torch.randn(96) for _ in range(2)]; forecast = pipeline.predict(context, prediction_length=720); print('Chronos forecast shape:', forecast.shape)" ; python train.py
